diagrams.title: PSR Bit Layout (CPSR/SPSR)
diagrams.description: Shows the 32-bit CPSR with flags, mode bits, and reserved bits
elements: Bits 27-24: Reserved (J, IT, Q, GE bits)
Bit 8-6: Control bits (E, A, I, F, T, M4-M0)
diagrams.purpose: Understand which bits control what functions in PSR

diagrams.title: Condition Codes Execution Flow
diagrams.description: Decision tree showing which condition code executes based on flag states
elements: Branch paths: EQ/NE check Z, CS/CC check C, MI/PL check N, VS/VC check V
AL always executes, NV is unpredictable
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how condition codes determine branch execution

diagrams.title: Compare Instructions Types
diagrams.description: Shows the four compare/test instructions and their operations
elements: CMP: Rd - Operand = result (flags set, result discarded)
CMN: Rd + Operand = result (flags set, result discarded)
TST: Rd AND Operand = result (flags set, result discarded)
TEQ: Rd XOR Operand = result (flags set, result discarded)
diagrams.purpose: Distinguish between CMP, CMN, TST, TEQ operations

diagrams.title: Exception Entry and PSR Saving
diagrams.description: Shows what happens to PSR when exception is taken
diagrams.purpose: Understand automatic PSR saving on exceptions
